title: edit: Despite the increasing interest and adoption of digital tools in Iran's healthcare, hospitals still face challenge…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: Despite the increasing interest and adoption of digital tools in Iran's healthcare, hospitals still face challenges in maintaining and equipping costly physical IT infrastructure to implement digital transformation, while the mechanisms through which this infrastructure influences digital transformation are still not well-understood. some hospitals have faced difficulties in implementing digital transformation successfully, despite possessing a robust physical IT infrastructure.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Despite the growth in the use of digital tools in Iran's healthcare sector, hospitals are still grappling with the task of maintaining and equipping expensive physical IT infrastructure to drive digital transformation efforts. Additionally, there is a need to better understand the mechanisms through which this infrastructure impacts digital transformation. Some hospitals have experienced obstacles in executing digital transformation initiatives, despite having a sturdy physical IT infrastructure in place.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: Despite the increasing interest and adoption of digital tools in Iran's healthcare, hospitals still face challenges in maintaining and equipping costly physical IT infrastructure to implement digital transformation. Despite the high cost of this infrastructure, the mechanisms through which this infrastructure influences digital transformation are still not well-understood. As some hospitals have faced difficulties in implementing digital transformation successfully, despite possessing a robust physical IT infrastructure. Therefore, it is crucial for Iranian hospitals to comprehend the mechanisms and roles of IT capabilities, including physical IT infrastructure, for supporting successful digital transformation.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Despite the growing adoption of digital tools in the healthcare industry in Iran, hospitals continue to experience challenges in maintaining and equipping costly physical IT infrastructure to enable digital transformation. Although this infrastructure comes at a high cost, there is still a lack of understanding about the mechanisms through which it influences digital transformation. Some hospitals have struggled to implement digital transformation initiatives successfully, despite having a robust physical IT infrastructure in place. Hence, it is vitally important for Iranian hospitals to understand how the various IT capabilities, including physical IT infrastructure, play a crucial role in supporting successful digital transformation.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: As some hospitals have faced difficulties in implementing digital transformation successfully, despite possessing a robust physical IT infrastructure.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: As several hospitals have struggled with implementing digital transformation successfully, even with a strong physical IT infrastructure in place.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: This study offers a twofold contribution. First, while physical IT infrastructure has long been viewed as a dimensions of IT capability in most studies, this study asserts that the advent of digital technologies, such as cloud computing, has altered this traditional perspective, particularly in the context of hospitals during the process of digital transformation. Within this framework, physical IT infrastructure is repositioned as an “IT resource,” with hospital-specific IT capabilities centered on IT architecture, IT human capability, and IT relationship capability. Second, although physical IT infrastructure in a hospital serves as a platform and shared resource for IT capabilities, it does not directly impact digital transformation. Instead, it has an indirect effect on digital transformation through strategic alignment and IT capabilities dimensions.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: This study provides a twofold contribution. Firstly, it challenges the traditional perception of physical IT infrastructure as a dimension of IT capability in most research. The study argues that the rise of digital technologies, like cloud computing, has shifted this perspective, especially in hospitals during the digital transformation process. Physical IT infrastructure is now considered an "IT resource," with hospital-specific IT capabilities focused on IT architecture, IT human capability, and IT relationship capability. Secondly, while physical IT infrastructure in a hospital serves as a platform and collaborative resource for IT capabilities, it has no direct impact on digital transformation. Instead, it indirectly affects digital transformation through strategic alignment and the dimensions of IT capabilities.
